sections.title: Your First Session
sections.content:
1. **Create Characters** using Book Two (or use the Pregen Crew in this book)
2. **Choose a Ship** (or use the Starter Ship, the *Dawnrunner*)
3. **Run Session Zero** to establish tone, safety tools, and crew bonds
4. **Start with Chapter 1 of The Gathering Storm** — or create your own opening scenario
5. **Play to find out what happens.** The dice, the choices, and the consequences will surprise everyone — including the GM.

---

> *"Roll the dice. Hope for the best. Prepare for the worst. That is life on Nexus 9."*

chapters.title: Session Zero: Building Your Campaign
chapters.content:
> *Before the first die is rolled, the crew must be forged. Session Zero is where you build the foundation that every session will stand on.*

---

sections.title: Purpose of Session Zero
sections.content:
Session Zero is a dedicated pre-campaign session where the GM and players collaboratively establish the tone, boundaries, characters, crew, and expectations for the campaign ahead. In Nexus 9, this session is not optional — it is essential. The game's themes of political tension, moral compromise, telepathic violation, and wartime trauma require explicit consent and calibration before play begins.

A well-run Session Zero accomplishes five things: it establishes safety boundaries, calibrates the campaign's tone, creates interconnected characters, builds the crew's shared identity, and generates the first threads of dramatic tension.

---

sections.title: Step 1: Safety and Consent
sections.content:
Before anything else, the table must agree on safety tools. Nexus 9 deals with themes that can be intense — authoritarian surveillance, psychic violation, war crimes, betrayal, and sacrifice. Every player must feel empowered to set boundaries.

**Recommended Safety Tools:**

The **Lines and Veils** system is the baseline. A Line is a hard boundary — content that will never appear in the game. A Veil is content that may exist in the fiction but will be handled off-screen or abstractly, never described in graphic detail. The GM should provide index cards and ask each player to write down any Lines and Veils anonymously.

The **X-Card** should be available at every session. Any player can tap or raise the X-Card at any time to indicate that the current content should stop immediately, no questions asked.

The **Open Door Policy** means any player can step away from the table at any time without needing to explain why.

| Tool | Function |
|:---|:---|
| **Lines** | Hard limits — this content never appears |
| **Veils** | Exists off-screen only — never described in detail |
| **X-Card** | Immediate stop on current content, no questions asked |
| **Open Door** | Any player may leave at any time without explanation |

---

sections.title: Step 2: Tone Calibration
sections.content:
Nexus 9 supports a range of tones, from political thriller to military drama to cosmic horror. The table should discuss and agree on where the campaign falls on several spectrums:

**Tone Spectrums (discuss and mark a position):**

| Spectrum | Left End | Right End |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hope vs. Despair** | Optimistic — heroes can make a difference | Bleak — survival is the best you can hope for |
| **Realism vs. Pulp** | Grounded — consequences are harsh and realistic | Cinematic — heroes are larger than life |
| **Politics vs. Action** | Intrigue-heavy — most conflicts are social | Combat-heavy — most conflicts are physical |
| **Mystery vs. Clarity** | Players discover the truth slowly | Players know the stakes from the start |
| **Personal vs. Epic** | Focus on individual character arcs | Focus on galaxy-shaking events |

The default Nexus 9 tone sits at: **Hopeful but pressured, grounded with cinematic moments, balanced between politics and action, mystery-forward, and escalating from personal to epic.**

---

sections.title: Step 3: Campaign Scope
sections.content:
The GM should present the campaign premise and discuss its expected scope:

**Duration:** How many sessions is the campaign expected to run? The Gathering Storm campaign is designed for 24–36 sessions across three arcs.

**Level Range:** Characters begin at Level 1 and can advance to Level 10 over the course of the full campaign.

**Starting Location:** All campaigns begin on Nexus 9 station. The GM should describe the station's five rings and ask players where their characters spend most of their time.

**Faction Focus:** Which factions will be most prominent? The GM does not need to reveal secrets, but should indicate whether the campaign will focus more on Terran politics, Valari mysticism, Aurelian intrigue, Kaelen underworld, or the Eclipse threat.

---

sections.title: Step 4: Character Creation
sections.content:
Each player creates a character using the rules in Book Two. During Session Zero, character creation should be collaborative — players should build their characters together, not in isolation.

**The Character Creation Checklist:**

1. Choose an **Ancestry** (Human, Valari, Aurelian, Kaelen, or Mixed Heritage)
2. Choose a **Community** (where you grew up — Station-Born, Colony-Raised, Fleet-Bred, Frontier, or Exile)
3. Choose a **Class** (Envoy, Operative, Pilot, Marine, Engineer, Mystic, Broker, or Medic)
4. Assign **Trait Scores** using the standard array or point-buy method
5. Select your starting **Domain Card Loadout** from your class's two domains
6. Choose an **Experience** — a defining life event that shaped your character (e.g., "Survived the Siege of Sol," "Escaped the Mindclave," "Witnessed a Luminary")
7. Write a **Secret** — something your character is hiding from the crew (see below)
8. Establish **Bonds** with at least two other player characters

---

sections.title: Step 5: Bonds and Secrets
sections.content:
**Bonds** are the connective tissue of the crew. Each player should establish at least two bonds with other player characters:

- **One Positive Bond:** "I trust [character] because..." or "I owe [character] a debt because..."
- **One Complicated Bond:** "I am suspicious of [character] because..." or "[Character] and I disagree about..."

Bonds are not static. They will evolve, strengthen, or break over the course of the campaign. The GM should revisit bonds at the end of each arc.

**Secrets** are private truths that each character carries. The player writes their secret on a card and gives it to the GM. The GM will weave these secrets into the campaign narrative. Examples:

- "I am an unregistered telepath hiding from the Mindclave."
- "I was ordered to spy on the crew by Aurelian intelligence."
- "I saw something in hyperspace that I cannot explain, and it is getting closer."
- "I killed someone during the war, and their family is looking for me."

---

sections.title: Step 6: Crew Creation
sections.content:
The crew is more than a collection of individuals — it is a shared identity with its own history, reputation, and resources.

**Step 6a: Choose a Ship.** The crew selects or is assigned a starship. For new campaigns, the Starter Ship (*Dawnrunner*, a modified Terran corvette) is recommended. Record the ship's stats on the Ship Sheet.

**Step 6b: Name the Crew.** Give the crew a collective identity. Are you a registered security contractor? A freelance troubleshooting team? An off-the-books intelligence cell? The crew's public identity affects how NPCs react to them.

**Step 6c: Establish the Debt.** Every crew starts with a Debt — an obligation that binds them together and to the station. The Debt creates immediate dramatic pressure. Examples:

| Debt | Description |
|:---|:---|
| **Financial** | The crew owes a significant sum to a Kaelen Syndicate lender. Payments are due monthly. |
| **Political** | The crew is indebted to a Terran senator who arranged their docking permits. She will call in favors. |
| **Criminal** | The crew is being blackmailed by someone who knows one of their secrets. |
| **Military** | The crew includes a deserter. If discovered, the entire crew faces charges. |
| **Personal** | A crew member's family member is being held as leverage by a faction. |

**Step 6d: Assign Crew Roles.** Each player chooses a primary role aboard the ship (Pilot, Gunner, Engineer, Comms/Ops, Captain, Marine). These roles determine who does what during starship encounters.

---

sections.title: Step 7: The First Thread
sections.content:
Before Session Zero ends, the GM should plant the first narrative thread. This is not a full adventure — it is a hook, a question, or an event that gives the players something to think about before the first proper session.

Examples:

- "As you settle into your berths on Nexus 9, a priority message arrives on your ship's comm: CARGO PICKUP — DOCKING BAY 14 — 0200 HOURS — NO QUESTIONS. The sender is anonymous."
- "A Valari priest approaches one of you in the Green Ring market and says, without preamble: 'The stars are fraying. You have been chosen to witness.' Then she walks away."
- "Station security informs you that your docking permit has been flagged for review. Someone with authority does not want you here."

---

sections.title: Session Zero Checklist
sections.content:
| Step | Complete? |
|:---|:---|
| Safety tools established (Lines, Veils, X-Card, Open Door) | ☐ |
| Tone calibrated across all five spectrums | ☐ |
| Campaign scope discussed (duration, level range, faction focus) | ☐ |
| All characters created with Ancestry, Community, Class, Traits, and Loadout | ☐ |
| Each character has at least two Bonds with other PCs | ☐ |
| Each character has a Secret (given to GM) | ☐ |
| Ship selected and Ship Sheet filled out | ☐ |
| Crew named and public identity established | ☐ |
| Crew Debt established | ☐ |
| Crew Roles assigned | ☐ |
| First narrative thread planted | ☐ |

---

> *"A crew that knows each other's strengths will survive. A crew that knows each other's secrets will either become unbreakable — or tear itself apart."*

chapters.title: Pregen Crew: The Dawnrunner Company
chapters.content:
> *Six ready-to-play characters for immediate use. Each pregen includes a full stat block, starting equipment, domain card loadout, bonds, and a secret. These characters are designed to work together as a crew aboard the starter ship Dawnrunner.*

> **Design Note:** These pregens use the Standard Array (+2, +1, +1, 0, 0, -1) and follow all character creation rules from Book Two. Their damage thresholds assume they are wearing their listed armor. Unarmored thresholds can be found in the Class Damage Thresholds table in Book Four.

---

## 1. COMMANDER KIRA VASQUEZ — The Envoy

*"Every word is a weapon. Every silence is a shield. And every handshake is a gamble."*

**Ancestry:** Terran
**Community:** Station Rat (Nexus 4 — before it vanished)
**Class:** Envoy (Codex and Grace Domains)
**Subclass:** Consul
**Level:** 1

### Traits

| Trait | Modifier |
|:---|:---:|
| Agility | 0 |
| Strength | -1 |
| Finesse | 0 |
| Instinct | +1 |
| Presence | +2 |
| Knowledge | +1 |

### Statistics

| Stat | Value | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hit Points** | 5 | Envoy base |
| **Stress Slots** | 7 | Envoy base |
| **Evasion** | 11 | Base 10 + Flight Suit bonus (+1) |
| **Armor** | Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1) | Thresholds below; Armor Score 3 |
| **Proficiency** | 1 | Roll 1 damage die |
| **Hope** | 2 | Starting session Hope |

### Damage Thresholds (Armored — Flight Suit)

| Minor | Major | Severe |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 5 | 11 | 20 |

### Starting Experiences

| Experience | Bonus | Source |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Street Smarts | +2 | Station Rat community |
| Negotiation | +1 | Chosen |
| Investigation | +1 | Chosen |

### Domain Card Loadout (2 cards)

| Card | Domain | Type | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Bureaucratic Loophole** | Codex | Action | Spend 1 Hope. Identify a legal or procedural exploit that forces an NPC to comply, delay, or reconsider. |
| **Silver Tongue** | Grace | Action | Roll Presence vs. Difficulty. On success, charm or persuade a target, shifting their disposition one step in your favor. |

### Starting Equipment

- Diplomatic credentials (genuine, Terran Coalition — flagged as "inactive")
- Formal attire (Aurelian-cut)
- Encrypted datapad
- Mag-Pistol (d6, Close range, Finesse)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Tenacity:* Once per Long Rest, when you fail an action roll, you may immediately reroll it and take the new result.

### Community Feature

*Station Knowledge:* You always know the fastest route between any two points on a station, and you can identify safe houses and bolt-holes without a roll.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Find out what happened to Nexus 4 — and whether the people aboard are still alive somewhere.

**Fear:** That the visions she has of Nexus 4 are not memories but warnings of what is coming for Nexus 9.

**Crew Role:** Captain

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "I trust Reth. He saved my life during the Frontier Incident, and I have never forgotten."
- *Complicated:* "Zara is hiding something. I can feel it every time she avoids my questions about her past."

### Secret

*Kira has fragmented memories of Nexus 4 — and in those memories, she sees the Eclipse. She believes the station did not vanish. It was taken.*

---

## 2. SERGEANT RETH TAL'VORI — The Marine

*"I do not fight because I enjoy it. I fight because someone has to stand between the darkness and everything I love."*

**Ancestry:** Valari (Vael'Kari — Warrior Caste)
**Community:** Military Brat (Valari Warfleet)
**Class:** Marine (Blade and Bone Domains)
**Subclass:** Vanguard
**Level:** 1

### Traits

| Trait | Modifier |
|:---|:---:|
| Agility | +1 |
| Strength | +2 |
| Finesse | 0 |
| Instinct | +1 |
| Presence | -1 |
| Knowledge | 0 |

### Statistics

| Stat | Value | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hit Points** | 6 | Marine base |
| **Stress Slots** | 6 | Marine base |
| **Evasion** | 10 | Base 11 + Marine Hardsuit penalty (-1) |
| **Armor** | Marine Hardsuit (Heavy, Tier 1) | Thresholds below; Armor Score 4 |
| **Proficiency** | 1 | Roll 1 damage die |
| **Hope** | 2 | Starting session Hope |

### Damage Thresholds (Armored — Marine Hardsuit)

| Minor | Major | Severe |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 7 | 15 | 26 |

### Starting Experiences

| Experience | Bonus | Source |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Tactics | +2 | Military Brat community |
| Intimidation | +1 | Chosen |
| Survival | +1 | Chosen |

### Domain Card Loadout (2 cards)

| Card | Domain | Type | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Cleaving Strike** | Blade | Action | Make a melee attack. On a hit, deal weapon damage to the primary target and half damage to one adjacent enemy. |
| **Tactical Withdrawal** | Bone | Reaction | When an ally within Close range is hit, spend 1 Hope. Move to their position and take the hit instead, using your own thresholds. |

### Starting Equipment

- Vibro-Sword (d10+3, Melee, Strength, Two-Handed, kinetic)
- Mag-Pistol (d6, Close range, Finesse)
- Marine Hardsuit (Heavy, Tier 1)
- Combat stim (x1, clears 2 Stress), extra ammo pack, breaching charge
- Soul-crystal pendant (religious significance)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Crystal Resonance:* You can sense the presence of Progenitor technology or Eclipse corruption within Close range without a roll.

### Community Feature

*Drilled:* In the first round of any combat, you act before any adversary, regardless of the spotlight order.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Protect his crewmates. After the dishonor of the Border Skirmishes, he will not fail the people who depend on him again.

**Fear:** That the Valari surrender at the Siege of Sol was not a mistake — and that his people are hiding something far worse.

**Crew Role:** Marine / Security

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Commander Vasquez earned my respect. She speaks truth even when it is dangerous."
- *Complicated:* "Mira's telepathy unsettles me. The Valari do not trust those who can read minds without permission."

### Secret

*Reth knows the true reason for the Valari surrender at the Siege of Sol. He learned it from a dying priest during the Border Skirmishes. He has told no one.*

---

## 3. ZARA "GHOST" OKAFOR — The Operative

*"I was never here. This conversation never happened. And you definitely did not see me take that datapad."*

**Ancestry:** Mindclave-Born (unregistered)
**Community:** Exile (Grew up in the Warrens of a frontier station)
**Class:** Operative (Midnight and Grace Domains)
**Subclass:** Shadow
**Level:** 1

### Traits

| Trait | Modifier |

| Strength | -1 |
| Finesse | +1 |

| **Silver Tongue** | Grace | Action | Roll Presence vs. Difficulty. On success, charm or persuade a target, shifting their disposition one step in your favor. |

### Starting Equipment

- Suppressed Pistol (d8, Close range, Finesse, Silent tag)
- Vibro-Knife (d6+1, Melee, Finesse)
- Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1)
- Lockpick set, holo-disguise emitter (1 use), smoke grenade (x2)
- Forged identity papers (3 sets)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Psychic Sensitivity:* You can read the surface emotions (not thoughts) of any creature within Very Close range as a Free Action.

### Community Feature

*Nothing Left to Lose:* When you are at 3 or fewer HP, you gain +1 to all action rolls.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Stay free. Stay hidden. Stay alive. And maybe, someday, find a place where she does not have to run.

**Fear:** That the Mindclave will find her — and that when they do, she will discover she is more dangerous than she ever imagined.

**Crew Role:** Comms / Operations

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Doc Patel patched me up when no one else would. I owe her my life — literally."
- *Complicated:* "Kira asks too many questions. If she finds out what I am, everything falls apart."

### Secret

*Zara is a rogue telepath (P4). She takes black-market inhibitors to suppress her abilities, but they are becoming less effective. The Mindclave has an active warrant for her capture.*

---

**Motivation:** Prove he made the right call when he disobeyed orders. Earn back his honor — on his own terms.

**Fear:** That the Kaelen Syndicate will call in the debt he owes them, and he will have to choose between his crew and his freedom.

**Crew Role:** Pilot

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Kel keeps the ship alive. Without them, we would be floating debris. I respect that more than anything."
- *Complicated:* "Reth is too rigid. Rules and honor are fine until they get people killed."

*The civilian transport Jace saved was carrying Kaelen Syndicate contraband. The Syndicate considers him an asset — and they have been calling in that debt.*

### Ancestry Feature

### Community Feature

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Understand the visions she has been having. Protect her patients. Find out if the Luminary was real.

**Fear:** That the visions are a sign of Eclipse corruption — and that she is already compromised.

**Crew Role:** Medic / Science Officer

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Zara came to me bleeding and desperate. I helped her without asking questions. That is what doctors do."
- *Complicated:* "Jace is reckless. One day his luck will run out, and I will be the one picking up the pieces."

### Secret

*Mira was contacted by a Luminary. She does not know what it means, but she has been having visions of the Eclipse's return — visions that are becoming more frequent and more terrifying.*

| **Heavy Swing** | Blade | Action | Make a melee attack with Advantage. On a hit, deal weapon damage +1d6 additional damage. |

### Starting Equipment

- Plasma Cutter (d8+1, Melee, Strength, can cut through bulkheads)
- Mag-Pistol (d6, Close range, Finesse)
- Marine Hardsuit (Heavy, Tier 1) — modified work suit
- Portable toolkit, welding torch, diagnostic scanner
- Salvaged Progenitor component (unknown function)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Resourceful:* Once per Short Rest, you may produce a common, non-weapon item worth 50 Credits or less that you "happened to have."

### Community Feature

*Self-Reliant:* During a Short Rest, you can repair one piece of damaged equipment without tools or an Engineer.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Understand the Progenitor component they carry. Build something that matters. Maybe even build a home.

**Fear:** That the Progenitor artifact is not a tool — it is a trap. And that by carrying it, they have already sprung it.

**Crew Role:** Engineer / Mechanic

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Jace trusts me with the ship. That is the highest compliment a pilot can give an engineer."
- *Complicated:* "Reth does not understand machines. He treats the ship like a beast of burden instead of a living system."

### Secret

*The Progenitor component Kel carries is not inert. It has been slowly interfacing with the Dawnrunner's systems, and Kel has noticed the ship responding to their thoughts. They do not know if this is a gift or a trap.*

---

## Crew Summary

| Character | Class | Role | Ancestry | Key Trait |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Kira Vasquez | Envoy | Captain | Terran | Presence +2 |
| Reth Tal'Vori | Marine | Security | Valari | Strength +2 |
| Zara "Ghost" Okafor | Operative | Comms/Ops | Mindclave-Born | Agility +2 |
| Jace Morrow | Pilot | Pilot | Terran | Agility +2 |
| Dr. Mira Patel | Medic | Science/Medical | Terran | Knowledge +2 |
| Kel-7 Torvik | Engineer | Engineer | Frontier-Born | Knowledge +2 |

---

> **Using These Pregens:** Each character is built using the Standard Array (+2, +1, +1, 0, 0, -1) and follows all character creation rules from Book Two. Their damage thresholds are listed with armor equipped. To find their unarmored thresholds, refer to the Class Damage Thresholds table in Book Four. Each pregen has 2 Domain Cards — one from each of their class's Domains. As they advance to Level 2 and beyond, they will gain additional cards from the Domain lists in Book Three.

> *"Six strangers. One ship. A station on the edge of the abyss. This is how legends begin — or how epitaphs are written."*

- *Positive:* "I trust Reth. He saved my life during the Frontier Incident, and I have never forgotten."
- *Complicated:* "Zara is hiding something. I can feel it every time she avoids my questions about her past."

### Secret

*Kira has fragmented memories of Nexus 4 — and in those memories, she sees the Eclipse. She believes the station did not vanish. It was taken.*

| Strength | -1 |

| Presence | 0 |
| Knowledge | +2 |

### Statistics

| Stat | Value | Notes |

| **Hit Points** | 7 | Medic base |
| **Stress Slots** | 5 | Medic base |
| **Evasion** | 10 | Base 9 + Flight Suit bonus (+1) |
| **Armor** | Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1) | Thresholds below; Armor Score 3 |
| **Proficiency** | 1 | Roll 1 damage die |
| **Hope** | 2 | Starting session Hope |

### Damage Thresholds (Armored — Flight Suit)

| Minor | Major | Severe |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 5 | 11 | 20 |

### Starting Experiences

| Experience | Bonus | Source |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Street Smarts | +2 | Station Rat community |
| Medicine | +1 | Chosen |
| Investigation | +1 | Chosen |

### Domain Card Loadout (2 cards)

| Card | Domain | Type | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Emergency Triage** | Splendor | Action | Spend 1 Hope. Touch an ally within Melee range. They recover 2 HP. |
| **Shield of Faith** | Valor | Reaction | When an ally within Close range takes a hit, spend 1 Hope. Reduce the severity of the hit by one step. |

### Starting Equipment

- Light Pistol (d6, Close range, Finesse)
- Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1)
- Advanced med-kit (heals 1d6+2 HP, 3 uses), surgical tools, bioscanner
- Station medical credentials, research datapad
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Tenacity:* Once per Long Rest, when you fail an action roll, you may immediately reroll it and take the new result.

### Community Feature

*Station Knowledge:* You always know the fastest route between any two points on a station, and you can identify safe houses and bolt-holes without a roll.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Understand the visions she has been having. Protect her patients. Find out if the Luminary was real.

**Fear:** That the visions are a sign of Eclipse corruption — and that she is already compromised.

**Crew Role:** Medic / Science Officer

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Zara came to me bleeding and desperate. I helped her without asking questions. That is what doctors do."
- *Complicated:* "Jace is reckless. One day his luck will run out, and I will be the one picking up the pieces."

### Secret

*Mira was contacted by a Luminary. She does not know what it means, but she has been having visions of the Eclipse's return — visions that are becoming more frequent and more terrifying.*

---

sections.title: 6. KEL-7 "KEL" TORVIK — The Engineer
sections.content:
*"Give me a wrench, a power cell, and thirty minutes. I will fix anything. Give me an hour, and I will make it better than new."*

**Ancestry:** Frontier-Born (Kaelen-raised)
**Community:** Frontier Settler (Grew up on a Kaelen salvage vessel)
**Class:** Engineer (Valor and Blade Domains)
**Subclass:** Artificer
**Level:** 1

### Traits

| Trait | Modifier |
|:---|:---:|
| Agility | 0 |
| Strength | +1 |
| Finesse | +1 |
| Instinct | -1 |
| Presence | 0 |
| Knowledge | +2 |

### Statistics

| Stat | Value | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hit Points** | 7 | Engineer base |
| **Stress Slots** | 5 | Engineer base |
| **Evasion** | 8 | Base 9 + Marine Hardsuit penalty (-1) |
| **Armor** | Marine Hardsuit (Heavy, Tier 1) | Thresholds below; Armor Score 4 |
| **Proficiency** | 1 | Roll 1 damage die |
| **Hope** | 2 | Starting session Hope |

### Damage Thresholds (Armored — Marine Hardsuit)

| Minor | Major | Severe |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 7 | 15 | 26 |

### Starting Experiences

| Experience | Bonus | Source |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Survival | +2 | Frontier Settler community |
| Engineering | +1 | Chosen |
| Technology | +1 | Chosen |

### Domain Card Loadout (2 cards)

| Card | Domain | Type | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Fortify Position** | Valor | Action | Spend 1 Hope. Create a barricade or reinforce a position. All allies within Very Close range gain +2 to their damage thresholds until the end of the scene or until the barricade is destroyed. |
| **Heavy Swing** | Blade | Action | Make a melee attack with Advantage. On a hit, deal weapon damage +1d6 additional damage. |

### Starting Equipment

- Plasma Cutter (d8+1, Melee, Strength, can cut through bulkheads)
- Mag-Pistol (d6, Close range, Finesse)
- Marine Hardsuit (Heavy, Tier 1) — modified work suit
- Portable toolkit, welding torch, diagnostic scanner
- Salvaged Progenitor component (unknown function)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Resourceful:* Once per Short Rest, you may produce a common, non-weapon item worth 50 Credits or less that you "happened to have."

### Community Feature

*Self-Reliant:* During a Short Rest, you can repair one piece of damaged equipment without tools or an Engineer.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Understand the Progenitor component they carry. Build something that matters. Maybe even build a home.

**Fear:** That the Progenitor artifact is not a tool — it is a trap. And that by carrying it, they have already sprung it.

**Crew Role:** Engineer / Mechanic

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Jace trusts me with the ship. That is the highest compliment a pilot can give an engineer."
- *Complicated:* "Reth does not understand machines. He treats the ship like a beast of burden instead of a living system."

### Secret

*The Progenitor component Kel carries is not inert. It has been slowly interfacing with the Dawnrunner's systems, and Kel has noticed the ship responding to their thoughts. They do not know if this is a gift or a trap.*

---

chapters.title: Starter Ship: The Dawnrunner
chapters.content:
> *She is not pretty. She is not fast. But she is yours, and she has never let you down — yet.*

---

sections.title: Ship Profile
sections.content:
The **Dawnrunner** is a modified Terran Coalition corvette, originally designated TC-CV-1187. She was built as a patrol vessel for the Terran Navy's frontier fleet, served for twelve years, was decommissioned after sustaining heavy damage during a border skirmish, and was sold at auction to a Kaelen salvage dealer. After passing through three more owners — each of whom added their own modifications and left their own scars — she ended up in the crew's hands.

The Dawnrunner is a workhorse: reliable, tough, and surprisingly versatile. She is not elegant, and her systems occasionally argue with each other due to the patchwork of Terran, Kaelen, and aftermarket components. But she can take a beating, carry a decent cargo load, and outrun most things her size.

---

sections.title: Ship Statistics
sections.content:
| Attribute | Value |
|:---|:---|
| **Ship Class** | Corvette (Light) |
| **Manufacturer** | Terran Coalition Naval Yards (modified) |
| **Length** | 48 meters |
| **Crew Capacity** | 6–12 (optimal crew: 6) |
| **Passenger Capacity** | 8 (in cramped conditions) |
| **Cargo Capacity** | 40 tons standard; 60 tons with cargo bay modification |
| **Hull Points** | 30 |
| **Armor** | 4 |
| **Evasion** | 12 |
| **Speed** | 3 (Moderate — faster than freighters, slower than fighters) |
| **Hyperspace Rating** | Class 3 (standard — 3 days per sector) |
| **Sensors** | Standard (Near range detection, passive scan) |
| **Signature** | Medium (detectable at Near range by military sensors) |

---

sections.content:
| Weapon | Damage | Range | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Twin-Linked Pulse Cannons** | 2d8 | Close–Near | Forward-mounted, requires Gunner action |
| **Point Defense Array** | 1d6 | Close | Automated, grants +2 to resist boarding and missile attacks |
| **Torpedo Launcher** | 3d10 | Near–Far | 4 torpedoes loaded; resupply at station (expensive) |

---

sections.title: Ship Systems
sections.content:
| System | Status | Description |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Engines** | Operational | Terran military-grade sublight drives. Reliable but loud. |
| **Hyperspace Drive** | Operational | Class 3 jump drive. Requires 10 minutes to calculate and spool. |
| **Life Support** | Operational | Supports 12 occupants for 30 days without resupply. |
| **Shields** | Patched | Kaelen aftermarket shield generator. Absorbs first 4 damage per round, then must recharge (1 round). |
| **Sensors** | Operational | Standard sensor suite. Can be boosted by Comms/Ops crew role. |
| **Communications** | Operational | Standard comms array with encrypted channel capability. |
| **Medical Bay** | Basic | A converted storage closet with a surgical table and basic supplies. Grants +1 to medical rolls aboard ship. |
| **Cargo Bay** | Operational | 40-ton capacity. Contains a hidden compartment (DC 16 to detect) installed by a previous owner. |
| **Brig** | Improvised | A reinforced storage room with a magnetic lock. Holds 2 prisoners uncomfortably. |

---

sections.title: Known Quirks
sections.content:
Every ship has personality. The Dawnrunner has more than most.

**The Hum:** The hyperspace drive emits a low, rhythmic hum when the ship is at rest. Long-time crew members find it soothing. New passengers find it unsettling. There is no mechanical explanation.

**Compartment 7:** A small maintenance compartment on the lower deck that is consistently 3 degrees colder than the rest of the ship. Previous owners reported hearing whispers. The current crew has learned to ignore it. (GM Note: This is a narrative hook. It may be connected to the Progenitor component Kel carries, or it may be something else entirely.)

**The Galley Dispenser:** The food synthesizer has a limited menu and an apparent preference. No matter what you order, there is a 1-in-6 chance it produces Kaelen spice porridge instead. The crew has stopped complaining.

**Mismatched Systems:** The Terran nav computer and the Kaelen shield generator occasionally conflict, causing brief system glitches during high-stress maneuvers. When the ship takes a Critical Hit, the GM may rule that one non-essential system temporarily goes offline until the Engineer makes a repair roll.

---

| **Engineering** | Lower deck, aft | Engineer |
| **Medical Bay** | Lower deck, midship | Medic |
| **Cargo Bay** | Lower deck, forward | Accessible to all |
| **Crew Quarters** | Upper deck, midship | 6 bunks, shared common area |
| **Airlock** | Port and starboard, midship | Boarding / EVA access |

---

sections.title: Upgrade Slots
sections.content:
The Dawnrunner has **3 Upgrade Slots** available. Upgrades can be purchased at stations or salvaged from wrecks. Each upgrade occupies one slot.

**Example Upgrades:**

| Upgrade | Cost | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Enhanced Sensors** | 500 credits | Extend sensor range to Far; +2 to scan rolls |
| **Reinforced Hull Plating** | 800 credits | +4 Hull Points |
| **Stealth Coating** | 1,200 credits | Reduce Signature to Low (detectable only at Close range) |
| **Expanded Cargo Bay** | 400 credits | Increase cargo capacity to 60 tons |
| **Improved Hyperspace Drive** | 1,500 credits | Upgrade to Class 2 (2 days per sector) |
| **Drone Bay** | 600 credits | Launch and control 1 reconnaissance drone |
| **Tractor Beam** | 700 credits | Grapple small objects or disabled ships at Close range |
| **Boarding Ram** | 500 credits | +2 to boarding actions; can force docking with unwilling targets |

---

chapters.title: Domain Card Quick Reference
chapters.content:
> *A summary of all nine domains, their thematic focus, which classes use them, and how domain cards work in play.*

---

sections.title: How Domain Cards Work
sections.content:
Each class in Nexus 9 has access to two **Domains** — thematic ability pools that define your character's capabilities. Your full collection of available cards is your **Vault**. Before each session, you select a subset of cards from your Vault to form your **Loadout** — the abilities you have ready for immediate use.

---

sections.title: Card Types
sections.content:
| Type | Icon | When Used | Cost |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Action** | ⚔ | On your turn, uses your action | Varies (Hope, Stress, or free) |
| **Reaction** | 🛡 | Triggered by a specific event | Varies (Hope, Stress, or free) |
| **Passive** | ✦ | Always active while in your Loadout | None (always on) |

---

sections.title: Card Costs
sections.content:
| Cost | Description |
|:---|:---|
| **Free** | No cost; simply declare use |
| **1 Hope** | Spend 1 Hope from the shared pool |
| **2 Hope** | Spend 2 Hope from the shared pool |
| **1 Stress** | Mark 1 Stress on your character sheet |
| **2 Stress** | Mark 2 Stress on your character sheet |
| **Action** | Uses your action for the turn |

---

sections.title: Loadout Rules
sections.content:
Your maximum Loadout size depends on your level:

| Level | Max Loadout Size |
|:---|:---|
| 1–2 | 4 cards |
| 3–4 | 5 cards |
| 5–7 | 6 cards |
| 8–10 | 7 cards |

You may swap cards in your Loadout during a **Short Rest** (swap 1 card) or a **Long Rest** (rebuild your entire Loadout).

---

sections.title: The Nine Domains
sections.content:
### 1. Arcana (Telepathy and Technomancy)

**Theme:** Psychic power, mind-reading, technomantic manipulation, and forbidden knowledge.

**Used by:** Mystic (Primary), Broker (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Psychic Probe, Technomantic Surge, Mind Shield |
| 2 | Neural Overload, System Hack, Thought Leech |
| 3+ | Mass Telepathy, Cognitive Override, Technomantic Storm |

---

### 2. Blade (Close Quarters Combat)

**Theme:** Melee mastery, aggressive strikes, and frontline dominance.

**Used by:** Marine (Primary), Engineer (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Cleaving Strike, Warrior's Stance, Riposte |
| 2 | Whirlwind Attack, Armor Breaker, Relentless Advance |
| 3+ | Executioner's Strike, Unstoppable Charge, Blade Storm |

---

### 3. Bone (Tactics and Evasion)

**Theme:** Battlefield awareness, evasive maneuvers, tactical positioning, and survival instinct.

**Used by:** Pilot (Primary), Marine (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Evasive Maneuvers, Battle Sense, Tactical Withdrawal |
| 2 | Flanking Fire, Danger Sense, Covering Fire |
| 3+ | Perfect Evasion, Tactical Supremacy, Ghost Run |

---

### 4. Codex (Law, Lore, and Bureaucracy)

**Theme:** Legal expertise, historical knowledge, bureaucratic manipulation, and information warfare.

**Used by:** Envoy (Primary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Bureaucratic Loophole, Historical Precedent, Diplomatic Immunity |
| 2 | Red Tape, Legal Precedent, Intelligence Briefing |
| 3+ | Executive Order, Classified Intel, Political Leverage |

---

### 5. Grace (Diplomacy and Deception)

**Theme:** Persuasion, manipulation, social maneuvering, and reading people.

**Used by:** Envoy (Primary), Operative (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Silver Tongue, Read the Room, Charming Deflection |
| 2 | Honeyed Words, False Flag, Social Chameleon |
| 3+ | Master Manipulator, Diplomatic Coup, Irresistible Offer |

---

### 6. Midnight (Stealth and Sabotage)

**Theme:** Infiltration, assassination, sabotage, and operating from the shadows.

**Used by:** Operative (Primary), Mystic (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Pick and Pull, Uncanny Disguise, Midnight Spirit |
| 2 | Shadowbind, Saboteur's Touch, Vanishing Act |
| 3+ | Death from Shadows, Ghost Protocol, Perfect Infiltration |

---

### 7. Sage (Survival and Logistics)

**Theme:** Navigation, resource management, wilderness survival, and supply chain expertise.

**Used by:** Broker (Primary), Pilot (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Wayfinder, Field Repair, Scavenger's Eye |
| 2 | Supply Cache, Astrogation, Emergency Rations |
| 3+ | Master Navigator, Resource Network, Survival Instinct |

---

### 8. Splendor (Medicine and Morale)

**Theme:** Healing, trauma care, psychological support, and crew morale.

**Used by:** Medic (Primary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Emergency Triage, Morale Boost, Steady Hands |
| 2 | Combat Medic, Trauma Surgery, Inspiring Presence |
| 3+ | Miracle Worker, Mass Heal, Unbreakable Spirit |

---

### 9. Valor (Protection and Heavy Weapons)

**Theme:** Defensive tactics, heavy weapons, shielding allies, and holding the line.

**Used by:** Engineer (Primary), Medic (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Shield Wall, Fortify Position, Suppressive Fire |
| 2 | Overwatch, Hardened Defenses, Heavy Barrage |
| 3+ | Impenetrable Defense, Orbital Strike, Last Stand |

---

sections.content:

| **Medic** | Splendor | Valor |

---

sections.title: Domain Overlap Map
sections.content:
Some domains are shared between classes, creating natural synergies and party composition considerations:

| Domain | Classes |
|:---|:---|
| Arcana | Mystic, Broker |
| Blade | Marine, Engineer |
| Bone | Pilot, Marine |
| Codex | Envoy (exclusive) |
| Grace | Envoy, Operative |
| Midnight | Operative, Mystic |
| Sage | Broker, Pilot |
| Splendor | Medic (exclusive) |
| Valor | Engineer, Medic |

**Exclusive Domains:** Codex (Envoy only) and Splendor (Medic only) ensure these classes have unique mechanical identities that cannot be replicated by other classes.

chapters.title: Appendix B: Telepathy Quick Reference

sections.title: The P-Rating Scale
sections.content:
Telepaths are rated on a 12-point scale (P1 to P12) by the Mindclave.

| Rating | Capability | Mechanical Equivalent |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **P1 - P3** | Surface empathy, sensing strong emotions, detecting lies. | Can roll Presence to read a room or detect deception. |
| **P4 - P6** | Surface thoughts, projecting short messages, mental static. | Can use basic Arcana cards (Level 1-3). |
| **P7 - P9** | Deep memory extraction, psychic attacks, mind-wipes. | Can use advanced Arcana cards (Level 4-7). |
| **P10 - P12** | Complete cognitive override, mass illusions, psychic screams. | Can use master Arcana cards (Level 8-10). |

sections.title: Telepathic Resistance
sections.content:
When a telepath attempts to read or manipulate an unwilling target, it triggers a **Psychic Contest**.

1. The telepath makes an Action Roll (usually Presence or Knowledge).
2. The target's Difficulty is their **Evasion + 2** (representing mental fortitude).
3. If the target is also a telepath, they can spend 1 Stress to increase their Difficulty by +2 for that roll.

sections.title: The Cost of Power
sections.content: Pushing telepathic abilities beyond safe limits causes physical trauma. A GM can offer a telepath a **Devil's Bargain**: "You can automatically succeed on this deep scan, but you must mark 2 Stress and take a Minor Scar (Nosebleed/Migraine)."

chapters.content: Telepathy in *Nexus 9* is not magic; it is a forced evolutionary trait that allows a user to perceive and manipulate the quantum states of other minds. While Mystics and Brokers use the Arcana domain to represent their trained abilities, any character with the "Telepath" Experience or a Mindclave background can attempt basic telepathic feats.

chapters.title: Glossary and Lexicon
chapters.content:
> *A comprehensive reference of terms, slang, and nomenclature used throughout Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark.*

---

sections.title: A
sections.content:
**Action Roll** — The core mechanic of the game. Roll 2d12 (one Hope die, one Fear die), add the relevant Trait modifier, and compare to the Difficulty. The higher die determines whether Hope or Fear is generated.

**Adaptive Content** — Under the DPCGL license, original content derived from or based on the Daggerheart System Reference Document that has been translated, altered, or transformed.

**Aethelgard** — A mysterious figure who emerged from a temporal anomaly approximately one thousand years before the campaign present. Aethelgard united the Valari castes, founded the Vanguard, and led the coalition that drove the Eclipse into hibernation. He vanished after delivering the cryptic warning: *"The cycle will break, or the cycle will break you."*

**Agility** — One of the six Traits. Governs dodging, piloting, acrobatics, and reflexes.

**Arcana Domain** — The domain of telepathy and technomancy. Primary domain of the Mystic, secondary domain of the Broker.

**Armor** — A defensive value that reduces incoming damage before it is applied to Hit Points.

**Aurelian Empire** — A declining hereditary aristocratic monarchy. Once the center of galactic commerce, now characterized by decaying infrastructure, opulent core worlds, and rebellious outer colonies. Known for ornate aesthetics in rich purples, golds, and crimson.

**Aurelia Prime** — Capital world of the Aurelian Empire, located in the Gilded Reach.

---

sections.title: B
sections.content:
**Blade Domain** — The domain of close quarters combat. Primary domain of the Marine, secondary domain of the Engineer.

**Blue Ring** — The diplomatic quarter of Nexus 9 station. Houses embassies, council chambers, and formal reception halls. Heavily secured and politically charged.

**Bone Domain** — The domain of tactics and evasion. Primary domain of the Pilot, secondary domain of the Marine.

**Broker** — One of the eight playable classes. A black-market dealer, information trader, and backroom negotiator. Operates through the Sage and Arcana domains.

**Burnwire** — Station slang for an encrypted, single-use communication channel used by smugglers and intelligence operatives.

---

sections.title: C
sections.content:
**Codex Domain** — The domain of law, lore, and bureaucracy. Primary domain of the Envoy.

**Communities** — A character creation element representing the cultural environment in which a character was raised. Provides mechanical bonuses and narrative hooks.

**Crew** — The collective term for the player characters and their shared ship. Crew creation is a collaborative process during Session Zero.

**Crew Roles** — Specific positions aboard a starship during ship combat: Pilot, Gunner, Engineer, Comms/Ops, Captain, and Marine.

**Cyclical Wars** — The recurring pattern of conflict between the Luminaries and the Eclipse, fought through proxy wars among the Younger Races over the course of a million years.

---

sections.title: D
sections.content:
**Damage Thresholds** — Tiered breakpoints that determine the severity of a successful attack. Minor, Major, Severe, and Critical thresholds are defined per class and level.

**Devil's Bargain** — A GM tool offering a player automatic success on a difficult action in exchange for a significant narrative cost, such as marking Stress or gaining a Scar.

**Difficulty** — The target number for an Action Roll, set by the GM based on the challenge's complexity.

**Director** — A high-ranking leader within the Mindclave hierarchy, each controlling a sector of operations.

**Domain Cards** — Ability cards organized by thematic domains. Each class has access to two domains. Cards are categorized as Action, Reaction, or Passive.

**Downed** — The state a character enters when their Hit Points reach zero. A Downed character is unconscious and dying unless stabilized by an ally.

---

sections.title: E
sections.content:
**Eclipse, The** — One of two remaining Elder races. The Eclipse believe that true strength is forged through conflict, hardship, and competition. They periodically awaken from hibernation on their hidden world, The Hollow, to incite galactic war. Their technology is crystalline and organic, favoring deep blacks, violets, and sickly greens.

**Elder Races** — Species that were uplifted by the Progenitors billions of years ago and built empires of staggering scope. Most departed the galaxy; the Luminaries and the Eclipse remained.

**Engineer** — One of the eight playable classes. A mechanic, inventor, and systems specialist who keeps the crew alive and the ship running. Operates through the Valor and Blade domains.

**Envoy** — One of the eight playable classes. A diplomat, spy, and manipulator who navigates the treacherous political waters of Nexus 9. Operates through the Codex and Grace domains.

**Evasion** — A defensive value representing a character's ability to avoid attacks entirely. Attackers must meet or exceed a target's Evasion to land a hit.

---

sections.title: F
sections.content:
**Faction Turn** — A GM-facing subsystem that tracks the actions and ambitions of major factions between sessions. Each faction has ratings for Military, Political, and Economic power.

**Fear** — The GM's resource currency, generated when the Fear die rolls higher on an Action Roll. Spent to activate enemy abilities, introduce complications, and escalate threats.

**Finesse** — One of the six Traits. Governs ranged attacks, lockpicking, sleight of hand, and precision work.

**Fraying, The** — The slow unraveling of galactic stability as ancient forces stir, alliances fracture, and the Eclipse's influence spreads. The central dramatic tension of the campaign.

---

sections.title: G
sections.content:
**Gold Ring** — The scientific and medical quarter of Nexus 9 station. Houses research laboratories, xenobiology facilities, and the station hospital.

**Grace Domain** — The domain of diplomacy and deception. Primary domain of the Envoy, secondary domain of the Operative.

**Green Ring** — The commercial and residential quarter of Nexus 9 station. The most densely populated ring, featuring markets, habitation blocks, entertainment venues, and the station's economic heart.

**Grey Assembly** — The tricameral governing council of the Valari, with equal representation from the Warrior, Religious, and Worker castes.

**Grey Ring** — The industrial and maintenance quarter of Nexus 9 station. Houses engineering bays, recycling plants, power systems, and the station's critical infrastructure. Also contains the entrance to the Warrens.

---

sections.title: H
sections.content:
**Handler** — A mid-ranking Mindclave operative who manages teams of Hounds and oversees telepathic operations in a given sector.

**Hit Points (HP)** — A numerical value representing a character's physical resilience. When HP reaches zero, the character is Downed.

**Hollow, The** — The hidden stronghold world of the Eclipse, where they enter deep hibernation between their cyclical awakenings. Contains buried caches of crystalline warships.

**Hope** — The players' shared resource currency, generated when the Hope die rolls higher on an Action Roll. Spent to activate domain cards, assist allies, resist consequences, and trigger class features.

**Hope Die** — The light-colored d12 rolled as part of every Action Roll. When it rolls higher than the Fear die, 1 Hope is generated.

**Hound** — A specialized Mindclave enforcement agent tasked with hunting unregistered telepaths (rogues).

**Hyperspace** — The method of faster-than-light travel in the Known Galaxy. Ships enter hyperspace through calculated jump points. Travel through hyperspace is not instantaneous and carries risks.

---

sections.title: I
sections.content:
**Imperial Conclave** — The Noble Court that governs the Aurelian Empire alongside the hereditary monarch.

**Instinct** — One of the six Traits. Governs perception, intuition, survival, and reading people.

---

sections.title: K
sections.content:
**Kaelen Syndicate** — A decentralized criminal empire operating through a network of merchant guilds, pirate fleets, and black-market cartels. Their aesthetic is sleek, angular, and predatory, favoring dark metallics, neon accents, and exposed machinery.

**Knowledge** — One of the six Traits. Governs engineering, medicine, history, hacking, and science.

---

sections.title: L
sections.content:
**League of Unaligned Worlds** — A loose coalition of independent systems, minor species, and frontier colonies that refuse to align with any major power. They seek representation and protection but lack military strength.

**Loadout** — The set of domain cards a character has equipped for a given session. Loadouts can be adjusted during downtime between sessions.

**Luminaries, The** — One of two remaining Elder races. The Luminaries believe in order, structure, and guided evolution. They secretly engineered human telepathy as a weapon against the Eclipse.

---

sections.title: M
sections.content:
**Marine** — One of the eight playable classes. A frontline soldier, boarding specialist, and heavy weapons expert. Operates through the Blade and Bone domains.

**Medic** — One of the eight playable classes. A combat physician, trauma surgeon, and morale anchor. Operates through the Splendor and Valor domains.

**Midnight Domain** — The domain of stealth and sabotage. Primary domain of the Operative, secondary domain of the Mystic.

**Mindclave** — The authoritarian organization within the Terran Coalition that controls all human telepaths. Led by Directors, staffed by Handlers and Hounds.

**Multiclassing** — The optional rule allowing a character to gain features from a second class at higher levels, subject to restrictions.

**Mystic** — One of the eight playable classes. A telepath, technomancer, and wielder of psychic power. Operates through the Arcana and Midnight domains.

---

sections.title: N
sections.content:
**Nexus 9** — The massive five-ringed torus space station that serves as the campaign's primary setting. Built as a diplomatic hub after the Terran-Valari War, it is the ninth attempt at the Nexus Project. Operational for approximately one year at campaign start.

**Nexus 4** — The fourth Nexus station, which was completed and became operational only to vanish without a trace, swallowed by an unexplained temporal anomaly.

---

sections.title: O
sections.content:
**Operative** — One of the eight playable classes. A specialist in stealth, sabotage, and targeted elimination. Operates through the Midnight and Grace domains.

**Orenthal** — The Firstborn of the Progenitors, the first sapient being in the galaxy. Chose to remain when the other Progenitors departed beyond the Rim.

---

sections.title: P
sections.content:
**P-Rating** — The Mindclave's twelve-point scale (P1 to P12) for measuring telepathic ability. P1–P3 covers surface empathy; P10–P12 represents complete cognitive override.

**Pilot** — One of the eight playable classes. An ace flyer, navigator, and tactical specialist. Operates through the Bone and Sage domains.

**Presence** — One of the six Traits. Governs diplomacy, deception, leadership, and telepathic force.

**Progenitors** — The first sapient species in the galaxy, naturally immortal. They nurtured the Elder Races before departing beyond the Rim. Only Orenthal remained.

**Psychic Contest** — The opposed roll triggered when a telepath attempts to read or manipulate an unwilling target.

---

sections.title: R
sections.content:
**Red Ring** — The military and security quarter of Nexus 9 station. Houses barracks, armories, the brig, and docking bays for military vessels.

**Rogue Telepath** — An unregistered telepath operating outside Mindclave control. Hunted by Hounds.

---

sections.title: S
sections.content:
**Sage Domain** — The domain of survival and logistics. Primary domain of the Broker, secondary domain of the Pilot.

**Scar** — A permanent narrative consequence gained when a character's Stress track fills completely. Scars represent lasting physical, psychological, or social changes.

**Session Zero** — The collaborative pre-campaign session where players establish tone, safety tools, crew bonds, ship selection, and campaign expectations.

**Siege of Sol** — The climactic battle of the Terran-Valari War, fought in orbit around Earth. The Valari achieved total military victory but surrendered unconditionally upon discovering that human and Valari souls are intrinsically linked.

**Splendor Domain** — The domain of medicine and morale. Primary domain of the Medic.

**Strength** — One of the six Traits. Governs melee attacks, lifting, endurance, and intimidation.

**Stress** — A resource representing mental and emotional strain. Marked when pushing abilities, resisting psychic attacks, or facing trauma. When the Stress track fills, the character gains a Scar.

---

sections.title: T
sections.content:
**Technoseers** — A secretive order of scientists and engineers who worship technology as a path to transcendence. They seek to recover and reverse-engineer Progenitor artifacts.

**Temporal Echo** — The hereditary Aurelian trait that grants brief, unreliable flashes of precognition.

**Terran Coalition** — The human federal republic. Recovering from the Valari War, it is sliding toward military authoritarianism under Mindclave influence.

**Threat Clock** — A GM tool for tracking the progress of major faction agendas. When a clock fills, a significant event occurs (invasion, coup, awakening, etc.).

**Tier** — A grouping of character levels that determines access to equipment, abilities, and domain cards. Tier 1 covers Levels 1–2; Tier 2 covers Levels 3–4; Tier 3 covers Levels 5–7; Tier 4 covers Levels 8–10.

---

sections.title: U
sections.content:
**Umbral Covenant** — A heretical splinter faction of the Technoseers that has begun experimenting with Eclipse technology, believing it holds the key to ultimate power.

---

sections.title: V
sections.content:
**Vael'Kari** — The Warrior Caste of the Valari.

**Vael'Sari** — The Religious Caste of the Valari.

**Vael'Tari** — The Worker Caste of the Valari.

**Valari** — A technologically superior, deeply spiritual alien species organized into three castes and governed by the Grey Assembly. Their biology features cranial ridges of crystalline bone and pale luminescent skin.

**Valor Domain** — The domain of protection and heavy weapons. Primary domain of the Engineer, secondary domain of the Medic.

**Vanguard** — A covert, multi-species order founded by Aethelgard, dedicated to watching for the Eclipse's return. Operates in secret across all factions.

**Vault** — The full collection of domain cards available to a character's class. The Loadout is drawn from the Vault.

---

sections.title: W
sections.content:
**Warrens, The** — The lawless, unregulated sub-levels beneath Nexus 9's Grey Ring. A maze of abandoned maintenance corridors, black-market bazaars, and criminal hideouts.

---

sections.title: Y
sections.content:
**Younger Races** — The species that evolved after the Elders departed, including humans, Valari, Aurelians, and Kaelen. They were caught between the Luminaries and the Eclipse in the Cyclical Wars.

---

sections.title: Station Slang
sections.content:
| Term | Meaning |
|:---|:---|
| **Burnwire** | An encrypted, single-use communication channel |
| **Clave-rat** | Derogatory term for a Mindclave loyalist |
| **Dirtside** | Planetside; anywhere that is not a station or ship |
| **Dustwalker** | A frontier explorer or prospector |
| **Fade** | To disappear, especially to avoid Mindclave detection |
| **Ghosting** | Running dark — operating without transponder or ID |
| **Gilt** | Aurelian currency; also slang for anything ostentatious |
| **Greyback** | A worker or maintenance crew member (from the Grey Ring) |
| **Hard burn** | Emergency acceleration; also slang for a desperate gamble |
| **Inkblot** | An Eclipse-touched individual or artifact |
| **Jumpsick** | Nausea and disorientation from hyperspace travel |
| **Rattler** | A Kaelen Syndicate enforcer |
| **Ringside** | On-station; the opposite of dirtside |
| **Scab** | A patch repair on a ship hull; also slang for a temporary fix |
| **Skinwalker** | A telepath who can alter their appearance |
| **Spinward / Rimward** | Directional terms on the station (toward the spin axis / toward the outer hull) |
| **Voidborn** | Someone born and raised in space, never having lived on a planet |
| **Warrens rat** | A resident of the Warrens sub-levels |

sections.title: Product Information
sections.content:
**Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark**
A cosmic-opera tabletop roleplaying game.

This product is an independent creation and is not affiliated with, sponsored by, or endorsed by Darrington Press or Critical Role.

---

sections.title: Compatibility Statement
sections.content:
This product is compatible with Daggerheart and is designed for use with the Daggerheart system. "Daggerheart" is a trademark of Darrington Press, LLC.

---

sections.title: License Attribution
sections.content:
This work includes Adaptive Content derived from the Daggerheart System Reference Document 1.0, which is Public Game Content published by Darrington Press, LLC.

The Daggerheart System Reference Document 1.0 is copyright Darrington Press, LLC, and is used under the terms of the Darrington Press Community Gaming License (DPCGL).

The original Public Game Content has been modified. The mechanical systems, stat block formats, domain structures, class frameworks, and action economy presented in this work are adaptations of the systems described in the Daggerheart SRD. All setting material, characters, factions, locations, narrative content, artwork, and original game content in this work are the creation of the authors and are not part of the Public Game Content.

The full text of the Daggerheart System Reference Document is available at: https://darringtonpress.com/daggerheart-srd

This content is licensed under the Darrington Press Community Gaming License. The full text of the DPCGL is available at: https://darringtonpress.com/license

---

sections.title: What This Means
sections.content:
- The game mechanics in this book (the 2d12 Hope/Fear system, action economy, domain card structure, damage thresholds, and character advancement framework) are adapted from the publicly available Daggerheart SRD.
- All original content — including the Nexus 9 setting, all factions, characters, starships, weapons, locations, campaign narrative, artwork, and supplementary game material — is the original work of the authors.
- This product is not official Daggerheart content. It is a third-party work created under the terms of the DPCGL.

---

sections.title: Intellectual Property Notice
sections.content:
Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark and all original setting material, characters, factions, locations, starships, artwork, and narrative content contained herein are the intellectual property of the authors. All rights reserved.

No part of this work's original content may be reproduced, distributed, or transmitted in any form without the prior written permission of the authors, except for brief quotations in critical reviews and certain other noncommercial uses permitted by copyright law.

---

sections.title: Art and Illustration Credits
sections.content:
All illustrations in this work were generated using AI image generation tools and are used in accordance with applicable terms of service. The art direction, prompts, curation, and integration of all visual assets were performed by the authors.

---

sections.title: Safety and Content Notice
sections.content:
This game contains themes of political conflict, war, espionage, psychic violation, authoritarian surveillance, personal sacrifice, and moral compromise. While the tone is ultimately hopeful, individual sessions may explore intense subject matter. The Session Zero chapter includes recommended safety tools (Lines and Veils, X-Card, Open Door Policy) that should be established before play begins.

---

sections.title: Acknowledgments
sections.content:
This game was inspired by the tradition of cosmic-opera storytelling — tales of civilizations in collision, ancient mysteries, and ordinary people caught in extraordinary circumstances. We are grateful to the creators of the Daggerheart system for building a flexible, story-forward mechanical foundation and for establishing a community license that enables independent creators to build upon it.

---

sections.title: Version
sections.content:
First Digital Edition — 2025

---

> *"The stars do not care about our treaties, our wars, or our prayers. But we care about each other. That is enough."*
